methods.name: Micro Precheck
methods.description: Run a short scored micro-calibrate baseline (2-5 minutes) at session opening to estimate readiness before TEACH.
when_to_use: Immediately after PRIME exits and before TEACH.
When readiness is unknown or likely drifted.
when_not_to_use: Never before PRIME for first exposure.
Skip only when emergency time constraints force direct review mode.
required_outputs: MicroCalibrateSnapshot (opening readiness signal for TEACH routing)

methods.name: Full Calibrate Probes
methods.description: Run the FULL CALIBRATE probe set after TEACH-close artifact to generate scored evidence for routing.
when_to_use: After TEACH close artifact is produced.
Before ENCODE method selection and retrieval scheduling.
when_not_to_use: Never as a replacement for opening MICRO-CALIBRATE.
Skip only when a validated full-calibrate result already exists for the same objective scope.

methods.name: Full Calibrate Priority Set
methods.description: Build a deterministic top-3 weakness set from FULL CALIBRATE telemetry to drive ENCODE method selection.
when_to_use: After M-CAL-002 completes.
Before selecting ENCODE methods.
when_not_to_use: If fewer than 3 valid items exist; run micro-recalibration first.
required_outputs: EncodeRoutingSeed (one ENCODE method-family recommendation per weakness)

methods.name: Story Confidence Tag
methods.description: After receiving the big-picture narrative overview, rate your confidence in the story as a whole. Can you retell the narrative thread? Where does the story break down? Light-touch calibration that respects top-down learners — no item-level quizzing.
when_to_use: After a narrative PRIME or TEACH story chunk
When learner prefers top-down big-picture processing
As an optional supplement before FULL CALIBRATE probes
when_not_to_use: As the only calibrate method in a first-exposure chain
After FULL CALIBRATE has already been completed for the same scope
When material has no narrative structure
required_outputs: Priority encoding targets (focus areas for ENCODE phase)
Optional probe seed suggestions for M-CAL-002

methods.name: KWIK Hook
methods.description: Structured 6-step encoding protocol for new terms. Sound (phonetic cue) → Function (true meaning) → Image (vivid visual tied to function) → Entwine (PIE method: personal meaning) → Resonance (learner confirms it clicks) → Lock (record as card/log). Each step is gated — don't skip ahead.

methods.name: Seed-Lock Generation
methods.description: Learner generates their own encoding hook BEFORE the AI offers help. Start with your own association, metaphor, or mnemonic. AI only assists if you're stuck. Enforces active generation over passive reception.

methods.name: Draw-Label
methods.description: Sketch the structure (anatomy, pathway, concept map) and label from memory. Fill gaps with source material.

methods.name: Teach-Back
methods.description: Explain the concept aloud as if teaching a classmate. Identify points where explanation breaks down.

methods.name: Why-Chain
methods.description: Ask 'why?' 3-5 times in succession about a concept to build causal depth. Each answer becomes the premise for the next question. Based on elaborative interrogation (Dunlosky et al.).

methods.name: Self-Explanation Protocol
methods.description: After reading each paragraph or concept, pause and explain WHY each step follows from the previous one. Supports silent, spoken, or written verbalization modes. Focus on reasoning, not restating facts. Merges Think-Aloud Protocol (formerly M-ENC-006).
required_outputs: Self-explanation notes or think-aloud log (per paragraph)

methods.name: Mechanism Trace
methods.description: Trace the causal mechanism step-by-step: what triggers what, and why. Build a cause→effect chain from input to output. Especially useful for pathophysiology and physiological pathways.

id_decode.runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests ENCODE, but runtime control_stage is TEACH.

methods.name: Concept Map
methods.description: Generate a learner-usable concept map that captures core nodes and labeled links for objective-scoped encoding.
methods.outputs_summary: Concept map (nodes + labeled links); Mermaid code
required_outputs: Learner-generated explanation or structured encoding artifact
Concept map with nodes and labeled links

methods.name: Comparison Table
methods.description: Create a side-by-side table comparing 2-4 confusable concepts across shared features. Highlight discriminating features. Builds differential diagnosis skill.

methods.name: Process Flowchart
methods.description: Draw a sequential diagram showing a process, pathway, or algorithm. Include decision points where applicable. Use Mermaid graph TD syntax for dashboard editor.

methods.name: Clinical Decision Tree
methods.description: Build a branching decision diagram: presentation → key findings → differential → tests → diagnosis. Scaffolds clinical reasoning into explicit decision points.

methods.name: Memory Palace
methods.description: Use spatial memory to encode ordered sequences by associating items with familiar locations. Based on the ancient Method of Loci used by Greek orators and memory champions.

methods.name: Chain Linking
methods.description: Create a bizarre narrative story connecting unrelated items. The absurdity makes it memorable without spatial locations.

methods.name: Hand-Draw Map
methods.description: Hand-draw a spatial mind map of the topic structure using own words, simple pictures, colors, and spatial positioning. Close eyes afterward to recall layout — positions trigger details. Max 5 minutes, never full rewrite.
when_to_use: After receiving a structural overview (H1 map or advance organizer)
When spatial positioning aids recall (anatomy, pathways, hierarchies)
First exposure to new material with clear structure
when_not_to_use: Material is purely procedural or sequential (use flowchart instead)
Time is severely constrained (<15 min total session)
Learner has already hand-drawn this topic recently
required_outputs: Hand-drawn spatial mind map (physical artifact, confirmed by learner)
Spatial recall confidence (can learner see layout with eyes closed?)
primary_citations: Van der Weel & Van der Meer (2024)

methods.name: Analogy Bridge
methods.description: Create an analogy linking this concept to something familiar. Test if the analogy holds at the edges.
required_outputs: Analogy statement (A is to B as X is to Y)
Breakdown points (where analogy fails and what that teaches)

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Clinical Application
methods.description: Apply the concept to a clinical scenario. Ask: how would this present? What would you test? How would you treat?
required_outputs: Clinical application narrative with specific patient characteristics
Presentation, testing, and intervention summary (all three addressed)
Verification notes (errors flagged against source material)

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Cross-Topic Link
methods.description: Identify 2-3 connections between this topic and topics from other courses. Look for shared principles.
required_outputs: Shared principles identified for each connection

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Side-by-Side Comparison
methods.description: Place two confusable concepts in a comparison table (features, functions, clinical signs). Identify discriminating features. Builds differential diagnosis skill.
required_outputs: Error list from initial memory-first attempt

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Case Walkthrough
methods.description: Walk through a realistic case to retrieve reasoning steps, identify gaps, and reinforce application accuracy.
methods.outputs_summary: Case walkthrough narrative; Assessment plan; Reasoning verification

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Illness Script Builder
methods.description: For a condition, build the illness script: (1) enabling conditions, (2) pathophysiological fault, (3) clinical consequences. Compare your script to the textbook version.
required_outputs: Complete illness script (enabling conditions, pathophysiological fault, clinical consequences)

id_decode.runtime_note: INT is an integration/interrogation family; use control_stage for runtime truth.

methods.name: Exit Ticket
methods.description: Answer three questions: (1) What did I learn? (2) What's still muddy? (3) What's my next action?
required_outputs: Muddy points or weak anchors identified

methods.name: Anki Card Draft
methods.description: Draft 3-5 Anki cards for the session's key concepts. Use cloze or basic format. Brain syncs to Anki.
required_outputs: 3-5 drafted Anki cards in canonical CARD block format
Each card tagged by topic and source
Cards staged in Brain for Anki sync
Card count logged to session record

methods.name: Drill Sheet Builder
methods.description: Build an interleaved drill sheet (30-60 timed items) and define cross-session validation checks.

methods.name: Post-Learn Brain Dump
methods.description: Close all materials. Write everything you remember from the session — stories, connections, details, anything. Then open materials, compare, identify gaps, and fill them in. Different from Free Recall Blurt: this is comprehensive session-level closure, not topic-level retrieval practice.
when_to_use: End of any learning session as closure activity
After encoding + retrieval blocks to consolidate
When learner wants metacognitive summary of what stuck vs. what didn't
when_not_to_use: Very short sessions (<15 min) where Exit Ticket suffices
Pure drill/speed sessions where Exit Ticket is better fit
required_outputs: Gap list (what was missing or wrong)
Session retention estimate (percentage of material recalled)

methods.name: Brain Dump
methods.description: Rapid orientation recall over existing notes to identify strong anchors and missing nodes before deeper work.
methods.outputs_summary: Connection refresh list; known-knowns snapshot; flagged gaps
when_to_use: Learner is returning to a topic with existing notes.
Tutor needs a fast re-orientation pass before ENCODE/REFERENCE.
when_not_to_use: Brand-new topic with no prior notes (route to M-PRE-010 then M-PRE-008).
High-anxiety learner who reads this as a test.

id_decode.runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.

methods.name: Overarching Pre-Question Set
methods.description: Generate 3-5 broad why/how/where-fit questions that jointly cover the whole selected material or module slice before deep study to set conceptual hooks.
when_to_use: New topic where conceptual framing is unclear.
Learner benefits from curiosity hooks before details.
when_not_to_use: Learner shows high anxiety with open-ended prompts.
Session time is too short to process questions meaningfully.
required_outputs: PreQuestionSet (3-5 broad conceptual prompts that jointly cover the full selected scope)

methods.name: Prior Knowledge Scan
methods.description: Conditional CALIBRATE method that maps known concepts to the new topic when prior notes or North Star context already exist.
required_outputs: Connection map (topic + related concepts with labeled links)

id_decode.runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.

methods.name: Hierarchical Advance Organizer
methods.description: Present a high-abstraction top-down framework (3-5 umbrella pillars + sub-branches) that still covers the full selected scope before detail study.
when_to_use: New, dense, or mechanism-heavy topics.
Learners who prefer big-picture-first orientation.
when_not_to_use: Learner already demonstrates expert-level schema familiarity.
Ultra-familiar topics where organizer adds redundancy.
required_outputs: PillarTree (3-5 pillars, max depth 2, collectively covering the full selected scope)

methods.name: Skeleton Concept Hierarchy
methods.description: Generate a bare concept hierarchy (topic core -> 4-6 umbrella categories -> short descriptors) that still covers the full selected scope without deep explanations.
when_to_use: Big-picture orientation for integrative topics.
Learners who think visually and need category scaffolding.
when_not_to_use: Pure procedural skill acquisition where step flow is primary.
Sessions with <3 minutes available.
required_outputs: SkeletonMap (central topic + 4-6 categories + descriptors that collectively cover the full selected scope)

methods.name: Structural Skimming + Pillar Mapping
methods.description: Rapidly skim headings/visual cues across the full selected scope, then map 3-5 umbrella pillars before deep reading.
when_to_use: Slide decks, textbooks, lecture packets.
Learners who need fast structure before details.
when_not_to_use: Highly spatial content where diagram-first review is required.
Sessions with fewer than 3 minutes total.
required_outputs: PillarMap (3-5 pillars with structural descriptors that collectively cover the full selected scope)

methods.name: Pre-Test
methods.description: Attempt to answer questions on the topic BEFORE studying it. Getting answers wrong primes the brain to encode the correct information more deeply.
required_outputs: Pre-test answers with confidence ratings for each item
Encoding priming record (questions kept visible for study phase)

id_decode.runtime_note: Historical ID prefix suggests PRIME, but runtime control_stage is CALIBRATE.

methods.name: Structural Extraction
methods.description: Extract a compact structural spine from source material before detail work. Build high-signal nodes and link every node to at least one objective.
when_to_use: Dense or complex modules where the learner needs a high-signal schema first.
Medical/STEM/exam-prep content with many interacting concepts.
when_not_to_use: Very short sessions where M-PRE-010 alone is sufficient.
Ultra-simple fact lists better handled with direct objective framing.
required_outputs: StructuralSpine (node count within node_cap, high-signal nodes only)
Objective linkage map (every node linked to at least one objective)

methods.name: Syntopical Big-Picture Synthesis
methods.description: Synthesize top-level structure across 2-3 sources into one module-level hierarchy with cross-links.
when_to_use: Multi-source study packets (slides + summary + glossary).
Clinical reasoning or research-heavy topics that need integration.
when_not_to_use: Single-source content where cross-source synthesis adds no value.
Time budgets below 3 minutes.

methods.name: Learning Objectives Primer
methods.description: Anchor the session to instructor-aligned learning objectives and choose module-wide or single-focus direction before deeper study.
methods.outputs_summary: ModuleObjectiveMap or FocusObjectiveCard; selected focus objective; learner acknowledgment
when_to_use: Session start for any new module/topic.
Re-entry after long gap where objective state must be re-anchored.
when_not_to_use: Never skip for first exposure; this is the mandatory PRIME gate.

methods.name: Terminology Pretraining
methods.description: Extract the names, abbreviations, and short component definitions that the learner must recognize before TEACH begins.
methods.outputs_summary: TerminologySet; abbreviation map; concise component definitions
when_to_use: Dense terminology, abbreviations, or nomenclature would otherwise make TEACH hard to follow.
First exposure to anatomy, neuro, or systems content with many named parts.
when_not_to_use: Terminology load is already light and obvious from the source.
The learner already has a stable working vocabulary for this topic.

methods.name: Big-Picture Orientation Summary
methods.description: Generate a short source-grounded overview that orients the learner to what the material is about without crossing into full explanation.
when_to_use: The learner needs a quick big-picture frame before details.
The material has a clear storyline, sequence, or purpose that can be summarized at a high level.
when_not_to_use: The learner is already using a stronger structural method and does not need an additional overview.
The requested move is really explanation-first TEACH rather than PRIME orientation.

methods.name: Ambiguity and Blind-Spot Scan
methods.description: Scan the selected source for ambiguities, unsupported jumps, contradictions, and follow-up targets that should be watched during TEACH.
when_to_use: The source is dense, compressed, or likely to skip reasoning steps.
The learner wants PRIME to surface where TEACH will need to slow down.
when_not_to_use: The source is already very explicit and low ambiguity.
The requested move is learner diagnosis rather than source-gap scanning.

methods.name: Error Autopsy
methods.description: Review errors from retrieval practice. For each error: (1) What did I say? (2) What's correct? (3) Why did I confuse them? (4) What cue will prevent this next time? Metacognitive error analysis.
required_outputs: Completed error autopsy table (all 4 columns for every error)
Root cause analysis for each error (Column 3 filled with specific conceptual confusion)
Discrimination cues created (Column 4 with actionable prevention strategy)
Anki card candidates identified from high-value errors

methods.name: Mastery Loop
methods.description: Re-study items missed during retrieval, then immediately re-test. Repeat until all items are recalled correctly. Based on successive relearning (Rawson & Dunlosky).
required_outputs: Mastery achieved on all targeted items (or partial progress with time noted)
Updated spacing recommendations based on loop performance

methods.name: One-Page Anchor
methods.description: Build a single-page reference anchor containing minimal definitions, decision rules, canonical examples, near-misses, and traps.
required_outputs: One-Page Anchor (definitions, decision rules, examples, near-misses, traps — all present)
Trap list and near-miss set (3 canonical + 3 near-miss examples minimum)

methods.name: Question Bank Seed
methods.description: Generate a mode-tagged seed bank of 10-20 retrieval questions from objectives and anchor artifacts.
required_outputs: Question Bank Seed (10-20 items, each mode-tagged and objective-tagged)
Coverage check draft (confirming all objectives have at least one question)

methods.name: Timed Brain Dump
methods.description: Close materials. Set timer. Write everything you remember about the topic. No peeking. Compare after. Timed constraint adds desirable difficulty.
required_outputs: Free recall dump with gaps marked

methods.name: Sprint Quiz
methods.description: Rapid-fire Q&A with Tutor. 10-15 questions in 5 min. Track accuracy for RSR.
required_outputs: List of missed questions with correct answers
Weak anchor flags (uncertain but correct items)

methods.name: Fill-in-Blank
methods.description: Review notes with key terms blanked out. Fill from memory. Targets specific vocabulary recall.

methods.name: Mixed Practice
methods.description: Interleave questions from 2-3 different topics in a single retrieval block. Builds discrimination and prevents blocked-practice illusion. Based on interleaving research (Rohrer et al.).
required_outputs: Confusion matrix (which topics got mixed up)

methods.name: Variable Retrieval
methods.description: Retrieve the same concept in 3 different formats: (1) free recall, (2) application question, (3) compare/contrast. Varied retrieval contexts build more flexible memory traces.
required_outputs: Three retrieval outputs (free recall, application, compare/contrast)

methods.name: Adversarial Drill
methods.description: Run near-miss adversarial prompts that force distinction between confusable concepts under low support.
required_outputs: Adversarial near-miss results with discrimination accuracy
ErrorLog updates (error_type and fix_applied per miss)

methods.name: Timed Sprint Sets
methods.description: Execute timed retrieval sprints and record latency per item to expose speed bottlenecks.
required_outputs: Timed sprint result set with per-item accuracy
ErrorLog updates (misses and speed failures tagged)

methods.name: Story Spine
methods.description: Teach a sequence or pathway as a compact story with a beginning state, trigger, causal progression, consequence, and clinical breakpoint.

methods.name: Confusable Contrast Teach
methods.description: Teach two confusable concepts side-by-side by naming the shared bucket, the key difference, the signature clue, and the classic trap.

methods.name: Clinical Anchor Mini-Case
methods.description: Use one tiny patient scene to show why the concept matters clinically without turning the block into a full case drill.

methods.name: Modality Switch
methods.description: Choose the best first representation for the concept and switch the explanation into that modality before overload sets in.

methods.name: Jingle / Rhyme Hook
methods.description: Compress a fixed ordered list or stable sequence into a short jingle or rhyme after the learner understands the meaning of the items.

methods.name: Depth Ladder (4-10-HS-PT)
methods.description: Route first-contact explanation through a brief hook, direct mechanism teaching, and DPT precision, using analogy or simple-language scaffolds only when comprehension actually needs recovery.
required_outputs: Fallback scaffold use or skip statement

methods.name: KWIK Lite
methods.description: Run the lightweight live mnemonic slot after the TEACH close artifact and before FULL CALIBRATE using one system-seeded cue plus one learner ownership action.

chains.name: Anatomy Deep Dive
chains.description: Anatomy-focused chain with a short PRIME orientation, a calibrate check, and drawing-led encoding before retrieval and overlearn.

certification.rationale: Selectable anatomy-focused template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Clinical Reasoning Intake
chains.description: Clinical first exposure with a short PRIME orientation before pre-test, illness scripts, comparison, retrieval, and overlearn.

certification.rationale: Selectable clinical intake template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Clinical Reasoning
chains.description: Build clinical reasoning chains with a short PRIME orientation, a calibrate scan, and contrast-heavy encoding before wrap.

certification.rationale: Selectable clinical reasoning template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Dense Anatomy Intake
chains.description: High-detail anatomy first exposure with a short PRIME orientation before pre-test, Draw-Label for spatial memory, retrieval, and later generative steps.

certification.rationale: Selectable dense-anatomy template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: DEPTH
chains.description: Pass 2: Selective mastery on high-priority objectives with a quick PRIME orientation, pre-test calibration, then depth encode/application work. Retrieval-driven. Cards only from errors.

certification.rationale: Selectable DEPTH template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Exam Prep
chains.description: Exam-focused chain with interleaving and error analysis. Prepare → Retrieve → Interrogate → Refine → Overlearn.

certification.rationale: Selectable exam-prep template chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: First Exposure (Core)
chains.description: Control-plane first exposure flow with explicit MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE opening, followed by ENCODE, REFERENCE, RETRIEVE, and OVERLEARN.

runtime_profile.mnemonic_policy: mnemonic slot only after TEACH-close artifact; full KWIK remains ENCODE depth work

certification.rationale: Selectable core first-exposure chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: First Exposure: Minimal
chains.description: Minimal first-exposure chain for low-energy sessions targeting definition and recognition modes. Uses compact opening sequence MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE, then light retrieval and wrap-up.

certification.rationale: Selectable minimal first-exposure chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: First Exposure: Procedure
chains.description: Procedure-focused first-exposure chain for high-energy sessions. Uses explicit opening sequence MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE, then process encoding, anchor generation, case walkthrough with fault injection, and timed sprint retrieval.

runtime_profile.mnemonic_policy: no default mnemonic slot in this chain unless explicitly inserted after TEACH-close artifact

certification.rationale: Selectable procedure-focused first-exposure chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Trey's Favorite: Start Here
chains.description: Default start-here chain for Trey. Standard first-exposure flow for medium-energy sessions targeting classification and mechanism modes. Opening is explicit MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE, then ENCODE, reference generation, and timed retrieval sprint.

runtime_profile.mnemonic_policy: mnemonic slot only after TEACH-close artifact; full KWIK stays in ENCODE depth work

certification.rationale: Selectable standard first-exposure chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Low Energy
chains.description: Low-effort chain for tired days. Short PRIME orientation, quick calibrate pulse, then reference, retrieve, and overlearn. Maintain streak without burning out.

certification.rationale: Selectable low-energy chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Mastery Review
chains.description: Deep consolidation with successive relearning. Retrieve → Refine → Overlearn.

certification.rationale: Selectable mastery-review chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Pathophysiology Intake
chains.description: Pathology first exposure with a short PRIME orientation before pre-test, self-explanation, concept clustering, retrieval, and refinement.

certification.rationale: Selectable pathophysiology intake chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Quick Drill
chains.description: Minimal time investment. Short PRIME orientation, quick calibrate pulse, then reference, retrieve, and overlearn. Good for spacing reviews.

certification.rationale: Selectable quick-drill chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Quick First Exposure
chains.description: Minimal intake chain when time is limited. Short PRIME orientation before pre-test, hierarchy setup, retrieval, and overlearn.

certification.rationale: Selectable quick first-exposure chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Review Sprint
chains.description: Fast review loop. Prepare → Retrieve → Interrogate (application) → Overlearn. Skips encode for known material.

certification.rationale: Selectable review sprint chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: SWEEP
chains.description: Pass 1: Fast structural understanding. Touch everything once. Produce visual maps, objectives, confusables, seed cards.

certification.rationale: Selectable SWEEP chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.

chains.name: Top-Down Narrative Mastery
chains.description: Top-down first-exposure chain with explicit opening order: MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE. TEACH builds a story + analogy bridge, closes with a compact anchor artifact, then full calibrate determines ENCODE depth. Teach-back is not a default live gate.

runtime_profile.mnemonic_policy: mnemonic slot only after TEACH-close artifact; full KWIK remains ENCODE depth work
runtime_profile.output_policy: notes and cards must be dependable session outputs

certification.rationale: Primary top-down gold-standard chain; must pass the stricter teaching-quality bar.

chains.name: Top-Down Forward Progress
chains.description: Top-down first-exposure chain with tiered exits and explicit opening order: MICRO-CALIBRATE -> TEACH -> FULL CALIBRATE. TEACH closes with a learner-usable anchor artifact before any mnemonic slot. Teach-back is removed from the default live path.

runtime_profile.mnemonic_policy: mnemonic slot only after TEACH-close artifact; full KWIK remains ENCODE depth work

certification.rationale: Structured top-down comparison chain; must pass the stricter teaching-quality bar.

chains.name: Visual Encoding
chains.description: Visualization-first encoding for topics with confusable concepts. Short PRIME orientation, quick calibrate pulse, then build visual representations before retrieval.

certification.rationale: Selectable visual-encoding chain; must pass end-to-end runtime certification.
